response: The internet originated from U.S. Defense Department networking research in the 1960s, with ARPANET’s first message sent between UCLA and Stanford in 1969 and a pivotal shift to TCP/IP in 1983. Tim Berners-Lee’s 1989 invention of the World Wide Web—introducing HTML, URLs, and HTTP—and the 1993 Mosaic browser opened the network to the public, fueling the late-1990s dot-com boom and 2001 bust, from which giants like Amazon and Google emerged. The mid-2000s rise of social media (Facebook, Twitter, YouTube), the smartphone-driven mobile web after the 2007 iPhone, and the spread of cloud computing (AWS, 2006) further transformed usage. Today the internet serves more than 5 billion people, underpinning global communication, commerce, and entertainment.
